project.name: BookRoom
project.description: Meeting room booking web application for office environments with Nuxt 3 frontend and FastAPI backend, PostgreSQL 16 persistence, JWT auth, role-based access, Docker Compose orchestration.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Project-level instructions for Claude Code agent: use Docker for testing, Chrome DevTools MCP for browser testing, write code comments in Russian.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Main project README describing BookRoom meeting room booking app architecture (Nuxt 3 + FastAPI + PostgreSQL), quick start with Docker Compose, test accounts, and feature overview for employees and admins.

nodes.name: Backend Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Dockerfile for FastAPI backend using python:3.12-slim base image, installs requirements and runs uvicorn with hot-reload on port 8000.

nodes.name: alembic.ini
nodes.summary: Alembic migration configuration defining script location, SQLAlchemy URL placeholder, and logging settings for the database migration tool.

nodes.name: env.py
nodes.summary: Alembic environment configuration that sets up async SQLAlchemy engine for database migrations, supporting both offline and online migration modes with asyncio.

nodes.name: run_async_migrations
nodes.summary: Async function that creates an async SQLAlchemy engine from config and runs database migrations through an async connection.

nodes.name: script.py.mako
nodes.summary: Mako template for generating new Alembic migration scripts with upgrade/downgrade stubs and standard revision metadata.

nodes.name: 5b9217162f4f_initial_tables.py
nodes.summary: Initial Alembic migration creating all core database tables: booking_settings, departments, rooms, users, bookings, and notifications with their relationships and constraints.

nodes.name: upgrade
nodes.summary: Creates six database tables (booking_settings, departments, rooms, users, bookings, notifications) with UUID primary keys, enums for user roles and booking statuses, and JSONB for room equipment.

nodes.name: downgrade
nodes.summary: Drops all six core tables in reverse dependency order to cleanly reverse the initial migration.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty Python package init file marking backend/app as a Python package.

nodes.name: config.py
nodes.summary: Application settings module using pydantic-settings to load database_url, secret_key, and access_token_expire_minutes from environment variables or .env file.

nodes.name: Settings
nodes.summary: Pydantic BaseSettings class defining database_url, secret_key, and access_token_expire_minutes (24h default) with .env file support.

nodes.name: database.py
nodes.summary: Database connection module creating an async SQLAlchemy engine and session factory, plus a get_db async generator for dependency injection.

nodes.name: dependencies.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI dependency injection module providing database session (get_db), JWT-based current user extraction (get_current_user), and role-based access control (require_role).

nodes.name: get_current_user
nodes.summary: FastAPI dependency that extracts JWT from cookies, decodes it, queries the database for the user, and raises HTTP 401 if authentication fails.

nodes.name: requirements.txt
nodes.summary: Python dependency list for the FastAPI backend including fastapi, uvicorn, sqlalchemy with async support, alembic, pydantic, jose JWT, and passlib for password hashing.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Docker Compose orchestration defining three services: PostgreSQL 16 database (port 5434), FastAPI backend (port 8000), and Nuxt frontend (port 3000) with dependency chain and persistent pgdata volume.

nodes.name: Implementation Plan
nodes.summary: Comprehensive 16-task implementation plan for the BookRoom meeting room booking system covering backend models, auth, CRUD, frontend pages, composables, and final integration.

nodes.name: Design Specification
nodes.summary: Technical design spec defining data models (users, rooms, bookings, notifications), REST API endpoints, role-based access (employee/manager/admin), and booking validation logic.

nodes.name: Frontend Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Dockerfile for Nuxt 3 frontend using node:20-alpine, installs npm dependencies and runs nuxi dev server on port 3000.

nodes.name: Frontend README
nodes.summary: Default Nuxt starter README with setup, development server, and production build instructions for npm, pnpm, yarn, and bun package managers.

nodes.name: tailwind.css
nodes.summary: Minimal Tailwind CSS entry point importing base, components, and utilities layers via @tailwind directives.

nodes.name: variables.css
nodes.summary: CSS design token definitions for the warm-orange theme with light/dark mode variables (20 tokens each), base typography using Plus Jakarta Sans, sidebar styling, card hover effects, and fade/slide animations.

nodes.name: components.json
nodes.summary: shadcn-vue configuration specifying default style, TypeScript mode, Tailwind CSS path, Lucide icon library, and component/utils aliases.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Frontend npm package manifest defining Nuxt 4, Vue 3, Pinia, shadcn-vue/reka-ui, Tailwind CSS, and VueUse as dependencies with standard build/dev/preview scripts.

nodes.name: robots.txt
nodes.summary: Permissive robots.txt allowing all user agents with no disallowed paths.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript configuration referencing Nuxt-generated tsconfig files for app, server, shared, and node environments.

nodes.name: .impeccable.md
nodes.summary: Design context document for Impeccable skills defining target audience (office workers), brand personality (warm/approachable), and design system tokens (Plus Jakarta Sans, warm orange palette, shadcn-vue).

nodes.name: main.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI application entrypoint that configures CORS middleware and registers all API routers. Exposes a health-check endpoint at /api/health.

nodes.name: models/__init__.py
nodes.summary: Declares the SQLAlchemy declarative Base class and imports all model classes so Alembic can discover them for migrations.

nodes.name: models/booking.py
nodes.summary: Defines the Booking SQLAlchemy model with status enum (pending/confirmed/rejected/cancelled) and relationships to User, Room, and Notification.

nodes.name: models/notification.py
nodes.summary: Defines the Notification SQLAlchemy model with type enum for booking status changes and relationships to User and Booking.

nodes.name: models/room.py
nodes.summary: Defines the Room SQLAlchemy model with JSONB equipment field, capacity, floor, approval requirement flag, and soft-delete via is_active.

nodes.name: models/settings.py
nodes.summary: Defines the BookingSettings model storing system-wide booking constraints: max duration, max days ahead, and minimum advance notice.

nodes.name: models/user.py
nodes.summary: Defines User and Department SQLAlchemy models with role enum (employee/manager/admin) and relationships to bookings, notifications, and department.

nodes.name: routers/__init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty init file for the routers package.

nodes.name: routers/auth.py
nodes.summary: Authentication router with register, login, logout, and me endpoints. Sets httpOnly cookies with JWT access tokens.

nodes.name: routers/bookings.py
nodes.summary: Bookings CRUD router with endpoints for listing, creating, cancelling, approving, and rejecting bookings. Enforces role-based access control for admin operations.

nodes.name: routers/departments.py
nodes.summary: Simple router exposing a single GET endpoint to list all departments ordered by name.

nodes.name: routers/notifications.py
nodes.summary: Notification router with endpoints to list current user notifications (limited to 50) and mark individual notifications as read.

nodes.name: routers/rooms.py
nodes.summary: Room management router with listing (filter by floor/capacity/equipment), schedule view, availability with 30-min slots, and admin CRUD with soft-delete.

nodes.name: routers/settings.py
nodes.summary: Settings router exposing GET/PUT for system-wide booking constraints. Creates default settings if none exist; updates require admin role.

nodes.name: routers/users.py
nodes.summary: Admin-only user management router with list, create, and update endpoints. Checks for duplicate emails on creation.

nodes.name: schemas/__init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty init file for the schemas package.

nodes.name: schemas/auth.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic schemas for authentication: RegisterRequest, LoginRequest, and UserResponse with from_attributes config.

nodes.name: schemas/booking.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic schemas for booking creation (BookingCreate) and response (BookingResponse) including user_name and room_name denormalized fields.

nodes.name: schemas/notification.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic response schema for notifications with type, message, read status, and booking reference.

nodes.name: schemas/room.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic schemas for rooms: create, update, response, plus AvailabilitySlot, BookingBlock, and RoomDaySchedule for timeline visualization.

nodes.name: schemas/settings.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic schemas for reading and updating booking system settings (max duration, max days ahead, min advance notice).

nodes.name: schemas/user.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic schemas for admin user management: UserCreate, UserUpdate, and UserListResponse.

nodes.name: security.py
nodes.summary: Security utilities providing bcrypt password hashing/verification and JWT access token creation/decoding using python-jose.

nodes.name: seed.py
nodes.summary: Database seed script that populates departments, users (admin + employees), rooms with equipment, and default booking settings if the DB is empty.

nodes.name: services/__init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty init file for the services package.

nodes.name: services/auth.py
nodes.summary: Authentication service layer with functions to look up users by email, create new users with hashed passwords, and authenticate credentials.

nodes.name: services/booking.py
nodes.summary: Booking service with validation logic: checks room existence, time-in-future, duration/advance constraints from settings, overlap detection, and auto-status based on approval requirement.

nodes.name: services/notification.py
nodes.summary: Notification service providing a single create_notification function that persists notification records to the database.

nodes.name: register
nodes.summary: Handles POST /api/auth/register: validates email uniqueness, creates user via auth service, sets JWT cookie, and returns user data.

nodes.name: login
nodes.summary: Handles POST /api/auth/login: authenticates credentials via auth service, sets JWT cookie on success, returns user data.

nodes.name: booking_to_response
nodes.summary: Converts a Booking ORM model to a BookingResponse schema, extracting denormalized user_name and room_name from relationships.

nodes.name: my_bookings
nodes.summary: GET /api/bookings endpoint that returns all bookings for the currently authenticated user, ordered by date descending.

nodes.name: all_bookings
nodes.summary: GET /api/bookings/all admin-only endpoint that returns all bookings in the system with eager-loaded user and room data.

nodes.name: create_booking
nodes.summary: POST /api/bookings endpoint that delegates to validate_and_create_booking service then reloads the booking with relationships for response.

nodes.name: cancel_booking
nodes.summary: PATCH /api/bookings/{id}/cancel that allows the booking owner or admin to cancel a booking, creating a cancellation notification.

nodes.name: approve_booking
nodes.summary: PATCH /api/bookings/{id}/approve admin-only endpoint that confirms a pending booking and sends a confirmation notification.

nodes.name: reject_booking
nodes.summary: PATCH /api/bookings/{id}/reject admin-only endpoint that rejects a pending booking and sends a rejection notification.

nodes.name: list_departments
nodes.summary: GET /api/departments endpoint that returns all departments as id/name pairs, ordered alphabetically.

nodes.name: list_notifications
nodes.summary: GET /api/notifications returns the 50 most recent notifications for the authenticated user, ordered by creation time descending.

nodes.name: mark_read
nodes.summary: PATCH /api/notifications/{id}/read marks a notification as read, ensuring it belongs to the current user.

nodes.name: list_rooms
nodes.summary: GET /api/rooms lists active rooms with optional floor, min_capacity, and equipment filters. Equipment filtering done in Python via JSONB field.

nodes.name: rooms_schedule
nodes.summary: GET /api/rooms/schedule returns all rooms with their bookings for a given date, used by the dashboard timeline view.

nodes.name: get_room
nodes.summary: GET /api/rooms/{id} returns a single room by UUID or raises 404.

nodes.name: get_availability
nodes.summary: GET /api/rooms/{id}/availability generates 30-minute time slots from 08:00-20:00 for a given date, marking each as available or booked with booking details.

nodes.name: create_room
nodes.summary: POST /api/rooms admin-only endpoint that creates a new room from the provided data.

nodes.name: update_room
nodes.summary: PUT /api/rooms/{id} admin-only endpoint that partially updates a room's fields using exclude_unset.

nodes.name: delete_room
nodes.summary: DELETE /api/rooms/{id} admin-only endpoint that performs a soft delete by setting is_active to False.

nodes.name: get_settings
nodes.summary: GET /api/settings returns booking system settings, creating defaults if none exist.

nodes.name: update_settings
nodes.summary: PUT /api/settings admin-only endpoint that creates or updates booking system constraints.

nodes.name: list_users
nodes.summary: GET /api/users admin-only endpoint that returns all users ordered by name.

nodes.name: create_user
nodes.summary: POST /api/users admin-only endpoint that creates a new user after checking for email uniqueness, using hash_password directly.

nodes.name: update_user
nodes.summary: PATCH /api/users/{id} admin-only endpoint that partially updates user role, department, or active status.

nodes.name: hash_password
nodes.summary: Hashes a plaintext password using bcrypt via passlib CryptContext.

nodes.name: verify_password
nodes.summary: Verifies a plaintext password against a bcrypt hash using passlib CryptContext.

nodes.name: create_access_token
nodes.summary: Creates a JWT access token with an expiration claim using HS256 and the app secret key.

nodes.name: decode_access_token
nodes.summary: Decodes and validates a JWT access token, returning the payload or None on failure.

nodes.name: seed
nodes.summary: Async function that seeds the database with departments, three users (admin + two employees), five meeting rooms with equipment, and default booking settings.

nodes.name: get_user_by_email
nodes.summary: Queries the database for a user by email address, returning the User or None.

nodes.name: create_user
nodes.summary: Creates a new User record with hashed password and optional department, committing to the database.

nodes.name: authenticate_user
nodes.summary: Authenticates a user by email and password, also checking the is_active flag before returning the user.

nodes.name: get_booking_settings
nodes.summary: Fetches BookingSettings from the database, creating default settings on-the-fly if none exist.

nodes.name: validate_and_create_booking
nodes.summary: Core booking creation logic: validates room, time-in-future, duration/advance constraints, overlap detection, auto-sets status based on room approval, and creates a notification.

nodes.name: create_notification
nodes.summary: Creates and persists a Notification record for a given user, booking, type, and message, flushing to assign an ID.

nodes.name: Base
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy DeclarativeBase subclass serving as the base for all ORM models in the application.

nodes.name: Booking
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy ORM model for bookings table with UUID primary key, date/time fields, status enum, and relationships to user, room, and notifications.

nodes.name: Notification
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy ORM model for notifications table with type enum, message text, read status, and relationships to user and booking.

nodes.name: Room
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy ORM model for rooms table with JSONB equipment, capacity, floor, approval flag, soft-delete, and bookings relationship.

nodes.name: Department
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy ORM model for departments table with unique name constraint and one-to-many relationship to users.

nodes.name: User
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy ORM model for users table with email, password hash, role enum, department FK, and relationships to bookings and notifications.

nodes.name: RegisterRequest
nodes.summary: Pydantic schema for user registration input with EmailStr validation, password, name, and optional department_id.

nodes.name: LoginRequest
nodes.summary: Pydantic schema for login input with EmailStr and password fields.

nodes.name: UserResponse
nodes.summary: Pydantic response schema for authenticated user data with from_attributes config for ORM compatibility.

nodes.name: BookingCreate
nodes.summary: Pydantic schema for booking creation input with room_id, title, date, start_time, and end_time.

nodes.name: BookingResponse
nodes.summary: Pydantic response schema for bookings with denormalized user_name and room_name fields and from_attributes config.

nodes.name: RoomCreate
nodes.summary: Pydantic schema for room creation with name, floor, capacity, equipment dict, and requires_approval flag.

nodes.name: RoomResponse
nodes.summary: Pydantic response schema for room data including equipment dict and approval/active flags with from_attributes config.

nodes.name: AvailabilitySlot
nodes.summary: Pydantic schema representing a 30-minute availability slot with start/end time, availability flag, and optional booking details.

nodes.name: BookingBlock
nodes.summary: Pydantic schema for a continuous booking block on the timeline with time range, title, and user name.

nodes.name: RoomDaySchedule
nodes.summary: Pydantic schema aggregating availability slots and booking blocks for a room's full day schedule.

nodes.name: SettingsResponse
nodes.summary: Pydantic response schema for booking settings with max_duration_minutes, max_days_ahead, and min_minutes_before_start.

nodes.name: SettingsUpdate
nodes.summary: Pydantic schema for partial settings update with all optional integer fields.

nodes.name: UserCreate
nodes.summary: Pydantic schema for admin user creation with email, password, name, role (default employee), and optional department.

nodes.name: UserListResponse
nodes.summary: Pydantic response schema for user listing with id, email, name, role, department_id, and is_active fields.

nodes.name: app.vue
nodes.summary: Root Vue application shell that wraps all pages with NuxtLayout and renders NuxtPage for file-based routing.

nodes.name: useAuth.ts
nodes.summary: Authentication composable providing login, register, logout, and session fetch functions that delegate to the auth store and backend API.

nodes.name: useAuth
nodes.summary: Composable factory that initializes the auth store and returns login, register, fetchMe, and logout methods for authentication flow.

nodes.name: useBookings.ts
nodes.summary: Booking management composable with CRUD operations for user and admin bookings, including create, cancel, approve, and reject actions via the API.

nodes.name: useBookings
nodes.summary: Composable factory returning fetchMyBookings, fetchAllBookings, createBooking, cancelBooking, approveBooking, and rejectBooking API methods.

nodes.name: useNotifications.ts
nodes.summary: Notification composable that fetches notifications from the API and delegates state to the notifications Pinia store.

nodes.name: useNotifications
nodes.summary: Composable factory that wraps the notifications store and provides fetchNotifications and markAsRead API methods.

nodes.name: useRooms.ts
nodes.summary: Room management composable providing fetch, create, update, delete, availability, and schedule API methods for meeting rooms.

nodes.name: useRooms
nodes.summary: Composable factory returning room CRUD methods (fetchRooms, fetchRoom, createRoom, updateRoom, deleteRoom) plus availability and schedule queries.

nodes.name: auth.vue
nodes.summary: Authentication layout with a split-panel design: warm dark branded panel on the left and a light form area on the right for login/register pages.

nodes.name: default.vue
nodes.summary: Default application layout composing the AppSidebar navigation and AppHeader with a main content slot for authenticated pages.

nodes.name: utils.ts
nodes.summary: Utility module exporting the cn() function that merges Tailwind CSS classes using clsx and tailwind-merge for conflict-free class composition.

nodes.name: auth.ts
nodes.summary: Route middleware that checks authentication state via useAuth, redirecting unauthenticated users to login and authenticated users away from auth pages.

nodes.name: role.ts
nodes.summary: Route middleware that restricts access to /admin routes by checking admin role via the auth store, redirecting non-admins to the homepage.

nodes.name: admin/bookings.vue
nodes.summary: Admin page listing all bookings with approve, cancel, and reject actions, using confirmation dialogs and the BookingCard component.

nodes.name: admin/rooms.vue
nodes.summary: Admin page for managing meeting rooms with a table view, create/edit dialog form, and delete functionality via the useRooms composable.

nodes.name: admin/settings.vue
nodes.summary: Admin settings page for configuring booking rules: max duration, booking horizon, and minimum advance time, with save confirmation feedback.

nodes.name: admin/users.vue
nodes.summary: Admin user management page with a table of users, role editing via select dropdowns, block/unblock confirmation dialogs, and a new user creation form.

nodes.name: addUser
nodes.summary: Validates required fields and sends a POST request to create a new user, handling errors and refreshing the user list on success.

nodes.name: confirmToggleBlock
nodes.summary: Confirms and executes a user block/unblock toggle by sending a PATCH request to toggle the is_active flag, then reloads the user list.

nodes.name: bookings.vue
nodes.summary: User bookings page with upcoming/past tab switching, booking list with BookingCard components, and a cancel confirmation dialog.

nodes.name: index.vue
nodes.summary: Main dashboard page showing a room occupancy timeline with date navigation, upcoming bookings list, and a quick-book CTA card.

nodes.name: loadSchedule
nodes.summary: Fetches the room schedule for the selected date via the useRooms composable and updates the schedule ref with loading state management.

nodes.name: showBookingDetail
nodes.summary: Displays a floating popover with booking details (title, user, room, time) positioned at the click coordinates on the timeline.

nodes.name: login.vue
nodes.summary: Login page using the auth layout with email/password form, error display, loading spinner, and navigation link to registration.

nodes.name: onSubmit
nodes.summary: Handles login form submission by calling the auth composable login method, managing loading state, and displaying API errors.

nodes.name: register.vue
nodes.summary: Registration page using the auth layout with name, email, password, and department fields, error handling, and a link to the login page.

nodes.name: onSubmit
nodes.summary: Handles registration form submission by calling the auth composable register method with user data, managing loading state and error display.

nodes.name: rooms/[id].vue
nodes.summary: Room detail page that fetches a single room by route param, displays room metadata and equipment badges, and embeds the BookingForm component.

nodes.name: rooms/index.vue
nodes.summary: Room listing page with filter controls via RoomFilters, room cards via RoomCard, loading spinner, and empty state for no results.

nodes.name: auth.ts (store)
nodes.summary: Pinia store managing authentication state with user data, loading flag, and computed getters for isAuthenticated and isAdmin status.

nodes.name: notifications.ts (store)
nodes.summary: Pinia store managing notification items with unreadCount getter and actions for setting items and marking individual notifications as read.

nodes.name: nuxt.config.ts
nodes.summary: Nuxt 3 configuration enabling Tailwind CSS, shadcn-nuxt, and Pinia modules, with SEO meta tags, Google Fonts, and backend proxy runtime config.

nodes.name: api/[...].ts
nodes.summary: Catch-all server API route that proxies all /api/* requests to the backend service using the BACKEND_URL runtime config.

nodes.name: tailwind.config.ts
nodes.summary: Tailwind CSS configuration extending the theme with shadcn-vue CSS variable-based color tokens, border radius tokens, and the tailwindcss-animate plugin.

nodes.name: BookingCalendar.vue
nodes.summary: Interactive timeline component (08:00-20:00) for visualizing room availability and selecting booking time ranges via click/drag with touch support. Renders existing bookings as blocks, a current-time indicator, drag-resizable selection handles, and hover previews.

nodes.name: BookingCard.vue
nodes.summary: Card component that displays a single booking with status badge, room location, date/time, and optional action buttons (cancel, approve, reject). Uses color-coded left border and badge styling per booking status.

nodes.name: BookingForm.vue
nodes.summary: Booking creation form that integrates BookingCalendar for visual time selection, provides duration presets (30min-2hr), manual time inputs, and submits via the useBookings composable. Fetches room availability on date change through useRooms.

nodes.name: AppHeader.vue
nodes.summary: Top header bar with breadcrumb navigation derived from current route, mobile hamburger menu toggle, current date display, notification bell, and user profile dropdown with logout. Uses useAuth for user data and route-to-label mapping for Russian breadcrumbs.

nodes.name: AppSidebar.vue
nodes.summary: Fixed sidebar navigation with BookRoom branding, a booking CTA link, main navigation links, and a conditional admin section shown only for admin users. Supports responsive mobile slide-in/out with overlay and auto-close on route change.

nodes.name: AuthPanel.vue
nodes.summary: Decorative left panel for authentication pages featuring animated geometric shapes, dot pattern background, and BookRoom branding with a tagline. Template-only component with scoped CSS animations, hidden on mobile viewports.

nodes.name: NotificationBell.vue
nodes.summary: Bell icon button with unread count badge that opens a dropdown listing recent notifications. Fetches notifications on mount and auto-refreshes every 30 seconds via the useNotifications composable.

nodes.name: EquipmentBadge.vue
nodes.summary: Small pill badge that renders an equipment type (projector, whiteboard, videoconference) with a matching icon and color scheme. Falls back to a generic gray style for unknown equipment types.

nodes.name: RoomCard.vue
nodes.summary: List-style room row (Notion-inspired) that links to the room detail page, showing room name, approval badge, floor, capacity, and equipment badges. Uses RoomEquipmentBadge for each enabled equipment entry.

nodes.name: RoomFilters.vue
nodes.summary: Filter bar with floor and minimum capacity dropdowns that emits filter parameters on change. Uses shadcn-vue Select components and auto-applies filters via watcher.

nodes.name: applyTimelineSelect
nodes.summary: Handles click/tap on the timeline to set or adjust the selected time range, checking for occupied slots and constraining to free intervals. Implements both initial selection (with 30-min default) and subsequent endpoint adjustment logic.

nodes.name: applyDrag
nodes.summary: Updates the start or end time of the selected range during a drag-resize operation, constraining the value to stay within free time boundaries and respecting a minimum snap interval.

nodes.name: formatDate
nodes.summary: Converts an ISO date string to a Russian human-readable label, returning 'Сегодня' or 'Завтра' for today/tomorrow and a short month format otherwise.

nodes.name: loadSchedule
nodes.summary: Fetches room availability slots and existing bookings for the selected date by calling fetchAvailability from the useRooms composable. Clears data on error.

nodes.name: onSubmit
nodes.summary: Handles booking form submission by calling createBooking with room, title, date, and time data. On success resets the form and reloads the schedule; on failure displays the server error message.

nodes.name: AlertDialog.vue
nodes.summary: Root wrapper for reka-ui AlertDialogRoot, forwarding all props and emits via useForwardPropsEmits.

nodes.name: AlertDialogAction.vue
nodes.summary: Styled action button for alert dialogs, wrapping reka-ui AlertDialogAction with buttonVariants styling.

nodes.name: AlertDialogCancel.vue
nodes.summary: Styled cancel button for alert dialogs, wrapping reka-ui AlertDialogCancel with outline variant buttonVariants and responsive margin.

nodes.name: AlertDialogContent.vue
nodes.summary: Alert dialog content panel with overlay, portal, and animated enter/exit transitions. The most complex alert-dialog sub-component with overlay and positioning logic.

nodes.name: AlertDialogDescription.vue
nodes.summary: Styled description text for alert dialogs, wrapping reka-ui AlertDialogDescription with muted foreground text styling.

nodes.name: AlertDialogFooter.vue
nodes.summary: Layout wrapper for alert dialog footer with responsive flex layout (column-reverse on mobile, row on desktop).

nodes.name: AlertDialogHeader.vue
nodes.summary: Layout wrapper for alert dialog header with flex column layout and responsive text alignment.

nodes.name: AlertDialogTitle.vue
nodes.summary: Styled title for alert dialogs, wrapping reka-ui AlertDialogTitle with semibold large text styling.

nodes.name: AlertDialogTrigger.vue
nodes.summary: Minimal pass-through wrapper for reka-ui AlertDialogTrigger with no additional styling.

nodes.name: alert-dialog/index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel file re-exporting all 9 alert-dialog sub-components (AlertDialog, Action, Cancel, Content, Description, Footer, Header, Title, Trigger).

nodes.name: Avatar.vue
nodes.summary: Root avatar component wrapping reka-ui AvatarRoot with configurable size (sm/base/lg) and shape (circle/square) variants via CVA.

nodes.name: AvatarFallback.vue
nodes.summary: Minimal pass-through wrapper for reka-ui AvatarFallback, displayed when avatar image fails to load.

nodes.name: AvatarImage.vue
nodes.summary: Wrapper for reka-ui AvatarImage with full-size object-cover styling for proper image display.

nodes.name: avatar/index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel file exporting Avatar sub-components and defining avatarVariant CVA function with size (sm/base/lg) and shape (circle/square) variants plus the AvatarVariants type.

nodes.name: Badge.vue
nodes.summary: Badge component rendering a styled div with configurable variant (default/secondary/destructive/outline) from badgeVariants CVA.

nodes.name: badge/index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel file exporting Badge component and defining badgeVariants CVA function with 4 variant options (default/secondary/destructive/outline) plus the BadgeVariants type.

nodes.name: Button.vue
nodes.summary: Button component wrapping reka-ui Primitive with configurable variant (6 options) and size (6 options) via buttonVariants CVA. Supports polymorphic rendering via as/asChild props.

nodes.name: button/index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel file exporting Button component and defining buttonVariants CVA function with 6 variant options (default/destructive/outline/secondary/ghost/link) and 6 size options plus the ButtonVariants type.

nodes.name: Card.vue
nodes.summary: Root card container component rendering a rounded bordered div with card background color and shadow styling.

nodes.name: CardContent.vue
nodes.summary: Card body content wrapper with horizontal padding and no top padding (p-6 pt-0).

nodes.name: CardDescription.vue
nodes.summary: Card description paragraph with small muted text styling for secondary information.

nodes.name: CardFooter.vue
nodes.summary: Card footer wrapper with flex row layout, centered items, and standard padding.

nodes.name: CardHeader.vue
nodes.summary: Card header wrapper with flex column layout and vertical gap spacing for title and description.

nodes.name: CardTitle.vue
nodes.summary: Card title component rendering an h3 heading with 2xl semibold tight-tracking typography.

nodes.name: card/index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel file re-exporting all 6 card sub-components (Card, CardContent, CardDescription, CardFooter, CardHeader, CardTitle).

nodes.name: Dialog.vue
nodes.summary: Root shadcn-vue Dialog component wrapping reka-ui DialogRoot primitive. Forwards props and emits to the underlying headless component.

nodes.name: DialogClose.vue
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper around reka-ui DialogClose primitive. Passes through all props to enable closing the dialog.

nodes.name: DialogContent.vue
nodes.summary: Dialog content panel wrapping reka-ui DialogContent with overlay, portal, centered positioning, animated transitions, and a close button with X icon.

nodes.name: DialogDescription.vue
nodes.summary: Wrapper around reka-ui DialogDescription that applies muted text styling. Used for descriptive text within a dialog.

nodes.name: DialogFooter.vue
nodes.summary: Layout component for dialog footer area with responsive flex layout for action buttons.

nodes.name: DialogHeader.vue
nodes.summary: Layout component for dialog header area providing flex column layout with centered text on small screens.

nodes.name: DialogScrollContent.vue
nodes.summary: Scrollable variant of DialogContent that enables overflow scrolling within the dialog panel. Includes overlay, portal, transitions, and close button.

nodes.name: DialogTitle.vue
nodes.summary: Wrapper around reka-ui DialogTitle applying semibold heading typography and tracking styles.

nodes.name: DialogTrigger.vue
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper around reka-ui DialogTrigger primitive that opens the dialog when clicked.

nodes.name: dialog/index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel file re-exporting all Dialog sub-components (Dialog, DialogClose, DialogContent, DialogDescription, DialogFooter, DialogHeader, DialogScrollContent, DialogTitle, DialogTrigger).

nodes.name: Toast.vue
nodes.summary: Shadcn-vue toast component wrapping reka-ui ToastRoot with variant-based styling (default/destructive) and swipe animations via class-variance-authority.

nodes.name: ToastAction.vue
nodes.summary: Shadcn-vue toast action button wrapping reka-ui ToastAction with destructive-group variant styling and focus ring.

nodes.name: ToastClose.vue
nodes.summary: Shadcn-vue toast close button wrapping reka-ui ToastClose with an X icon from lucide-vue-next, opacity transitions on hover/focus, and destructive variant colors.

nodes.name: ToastDescription.vue
nodes.summary: Shadcn-vue toast description text wrapping reka-ui ToastDescription with small text and reduced opacity styling.

nodes.name: ToastProvider.vue
nodes.summary: Shadcn-vue toast provider wrapping reka-ui ToastProvider to establish the toast context at the root of the toast system.

nodes.name: ToastTitle.vue
nodes.summary: Shadcn-vue toast title wrapping reka-ui ToastTitle with semibold text styling.

nodes.name: ToastViewport.vue
nodes.summary: Shadcn-vue toast viewport wrapping reka-ui ToastViewport, positioned fixed at bottom-right to stack toast notifications.

nodes.name: Toaster.vue
nodes.summary: Orchestrator component that renders active toasts from useToast composable, handling both string and VNode descriptions with ToastProvider, Toast, ToastClose, and ToastViewport.

nodes.name: toast/index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel file re-exporting all Toast sub-components, useToast composable, toast function, toastVariants styling, and ToastProps type. Also defines toast variant styles via class-variance-authority.

nodes.name: use-toast.ts
nodes.summary: Composable managing global toast notification state with a reducer-style dispatch pattern. Handles add, update, dismiss, and remove actions with auto-removal queue and a configurable toast limit.

nodes.name: dispatch
nodes.summary: Reducer-style dispatch function handling four toast action types (ADD, UPDATE, DISMISS, REMOVE) to mutate the reactive toast state array.

nodes.name: toast
nodes.summary: Factory function that creates a new toast notification with a unique ID, dispatches ADD_TOAST, and returns update/dismiss handles.

nodes.name: addToRemoveQueue
nodes.summary: Schedules delayed removal of a toast by setting a timeout that dispatches REMOVE_TOAST, preventing duplicate timers for the same toast.

layers.name: Backend API
layers.description: FastAPI routers and endpoint definitions for auth, bookings, rooms, users, notifications, departments, and settings. The HTTP interface layer of the BookRoom backend.

layers.name: Backend Services
layers.description: Business logic services (auth, booking, notification), security utilities, dependency injection, app entry point (main.py), and database seeding for the BookRoom backend.

layers.name: Backend Data
layers.description: SQLAlchemy ORM models (user, room, booking, notification, settings), Pydantic schemas for request/response validation, database connection setup, Alembic migrations, and migration configuration.

layers.name: Frontend Pages & Layouts
layers.description: Nuxt 3 page components for all routes (dashboard, rooms, bookings, login, register, admin pages), layout shells (default and auth), and the root app.vue entry point.

layers.name: Frontend Feature Components
layers.description: Domain-specific Vue components organized by feature: booking (BookingCalendar, BookingCard, BookingForm), layout (AppHeader, AppSidebar, AuthPanel, NotificationBell), and room (EquipmentBadge, RoomCard, RoomFilters).

layers.name: Frontend Service Layer
layers.description: Composables (useAuth, useBookings, useNotifications, useRooms), Pinia stores (auth, notifications), route middleware (auth, role), API proxy server handler, and shared utility functions.

layers.name: Frontend UI Library
layers.description: shadcn-vue component wrappers: AlertDialog, Avatar, Badge, Button, Card, Dialog, DropdownMenu, Input, Label, Select, Separator, Sheet, Table, Tabs, Toast — providing the foundational design system for BookRoom.

layers.name: Infrastructure & Configuration
layers.description: Docker build files (backend/frontend Dockerfiles, docker-compose.yml), project configs (nuxt.config, tailwind.config, tsconfig, package.json, components.json, alembic.ini, requirements.txt), and CSS assets (Tailwind base styles, CSS custom properties).

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project README, frontend README, implementation plans, design specs, CLAUDE.md agent instructions, .impeccable.md design context, and robots.txt.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: The README is the single best starting point for understanding BookRoom. It describes the full-stack architecture (Nuxt 3 + FastAPI + PostgreSQL), explains how to launch everything with Docker Compose, lists test accounts, and summarizes the feature set for employees and admins. Read this before touching any code.
tour.languageLesson: Project READMEs serve as the human entry point to a codebase. CLAUDE.md is a newer convention that provides AI-specific instructions for working with the project.

tour.title: Infrastructure & Deployment
tour.description: Docker Compose orchestrates three services: PostgreSQL 16 (port 5434), the FastAPI backend (port 8000), and the Nuxt frontend (port 3000). Each service has its own Dockerfile. The backend uses python:3.12-slim with uvicorn, while the frontend uses node:20-alpine with nuxi dev. This is where you understand how the system runs as a whole.
tour.languageLesson: Docker Compose 'depends_on' creates a startup ordering chain: database starts first, then backend, then frontend. Each Dockerfile defines an isolated build context with its own dependency installation.

tour.title: Backend Entry Point
tour.description: main.py is the FastAPI application root. It creates the FastAPI instance, configures CORS middleware for cross-origin requests, and registers all seven API routers (auth, rooms, bookings, users, notifications, settings, departments). With a fan-out of 7 router imports, it is the single hub that connects the entire backend API surface.
tour.languageLesson: FastAPI uses the 'include_router' pattern to compose modular route groups into a single application. The config module uses pydantic-settings to load environment variables with type validation.

tour.title: Backend Data Models
tour.description: The data layer forms a tightly coupled cluster: models/__init__.py declares the SQLAlchemy Base class and imports all five model files, while each model file imports Base back. The User model (fan-in: 10) is the most referenced node in the entire backend -- nearly every router and service depends on it. The models define User, Room, Booking, Notification, and BookingSettings with their relationships and constraints.
tour.languageLesson: SQLAlchemy's declarative Base pattern requires all models to inherit from a shared Base class. The circular import between __init__.py and individual models is intentional -- it ensures Alembic can auto-discover all tables for migration generation.

tour.title: Backend API Layer
tour.description: The routers are the highest fan-out nodes in the backend: bookings.py (13 edges) handles CRUD plus approve/reject workflows, rooms.py (11 edges) manages listings, schedules, and availability slots. The auth router handles JWT-based login/register with httpOnly cookies. Together these three routers define the core user-facing API surface.
tour.languageLesson: FastAPI routers use Python type hints and Depends() for automatic request validation, dependency injection, and OpenAPI documentation generation. Each router is a self-contained module with its own prefix and tags.

tour.title: Backend Business Logic & Security
tour.description: The service layer separates business rules from HTTP concerns. The booking service validates time constraints, checks for overlaps, and auto-determines booking status. The security module (fan-in: 5) provides bcrypt password hashing and JWT token management. The dependencies module wires database sessions and current-user extraction into every protected endpoint.
tour.languageLesson: FastAPI's Depends() creates a dependency injection tree. get_db yields a database session, get_current_user decodes the JWT, and require_role checks authorization -- these chain together so each endpoint declaratively states its requirements.

tour.title: Frontend Entry Point & Configuration
tour.description: app.vue is the root Vue component that wraps all pages with NuxtLayout and NuxtPage for file-based routing. nuxt.config.ts enables the Tailwind CSS, shadcn-nuxt, and Pinia modules, configures SEO meta tags, and sets up the backend API proxy. The tailwind.config extends the theme with shadcn-vue CSS variable-based design tokens.
tour.languageLesson: Nuxt 3 uses convention-based architecture: pages/ for routes, layouts/ for page shells, composables/ for shared logic, and middleware/ for route guards. The nuxt.config.ts modules array is the extension point for integrating UI libraries.

tour.title: Frontend Pages & Routing
tour.description: The pages directory maps directly to URL routes via Nuxt file-based routing. index.vue is the dashboard with a room occupancy timeline. The rooms/ directory has a list page and a dynamic [id].vue detail page. Admin pages (bookings, rooms, settings, users) are nested under admin/. Login and register pages use a separate auth layout with a decorative split-panel design.
tour.languageLesson: Nuxt file-based routing maps filesystem paths to URLs: pages/rooms/[id].vue becomes /rooms/:id. Square brackets denote dynamic route parameters. The definePageMeta macro declares middleware requirements per page.

tour.title: Frontend State & Composables
tour.description: useAuth.ts is the highest fan-in node in the entire graph (12 incoming edges) -- every page, layout component, and middleware depends on it. Composables wrap API calls and delegate state to Pinia stores. The auth store tracks user data and computed isAuthenticated/isAdmin getters. Route middleware (auth.ts, role.ts) guard protected pages by checking these stores.
tour.languageLesson: Vue 3 composables (use* functions) encapsulate reactive state and side effects as reusable units. Pinia stores provide centralized state that persists across component lifecycles. This two-layer pattern keeps API logic in composables and shared state in stores.

tour.title: Frontend Feature Components
tour.description: BookingCalendar is the most complex frontend component: an interactive 08:00-20:00 timeline with click-drag time selection, touch support, and existing booking visualization. BookingForm integrates the calendar with duration presets and submits via useBookings. RoomCard renders room rows with equipment badges. AppSidebar provides the navigation shell with responsive mobile support.
tour.languageLesson: Vue Single File Components (.vue) co-locate template, script, and styles. The BookingForm-to-BookingCalendar relationship shows parent-child composition: the form manages data flow while the calendar handles interactive visualization.

tour.title: Frontend UI Library (shadcn-vue)
tour.description: The project uses shadcn-vue, which provides copy-paste UI primitives built on reka-ui headless components. Each UI component directory (button, card, dialog, table, toast, etc.) has individual .vue files and a barrel index.ts. The dropdown-menu barrel file has the highest fan-out (14 exports) in the frontend. These components are not installed from npm -- they live in the codebase and are fully customizable.
tour.languageLesson: shadcn-vue follows the 'copy-paste component library' pattern: instead of importing from node_modules, components are scaffolded into your project so you own the code. The components.json configures how the shadcn CLI generates components with your project's conventions.

tour.title: Database Migrations & Design Docs
tour.description: Alembic manages database schema evolution. The initial migration creates all core tables (booking_settings, departments, rooms, users, bookings, notifications) in a single step. The seed script populates test data. The design spec and implementation plan in docs/ trace the full journey from requirements to code, showing how the 16-task plan mapped to the final architecture.
tour.languageLesson: Alembic generates versioned migration scripts that can upgrade or downgrade the database schema. The env.py configures async SQLAlchemy support. Keeping design specs alongside code provides traceability from requirements through implementation.
